VAL-CROSS-001.evidence: Registered a new user at /register, redirected to /dashboard. Clicked Logout, redirected to /login. Logged back in with the same credentials, redirected back to /dashboard. Verified through agent-browser taking screenshots (register.png, dashboard_after_register.png, login_after_logout.png, dashboard_after_login.png).

VAL-CROSS-002.evidence: Ran `cd /Users/pierrebeunardeau/GalileoLuxury && pnpm build` which successfully built the project and its packages. Using `grep` identified that `@galileo/shared` is imported in `apps/api/src/routes/auth/register.ts` and `apps/api/src/routes/auth/login.ts` for schemas, resolving fine at build time.

VAL-CROSS-003.evidence: The browser flow correctly logged in and registered using the UI which communicates with the API. CORS is correctly configured as tested by a curl OPTIONS preflight request to `http://localhost:4000/auth/login` returning `access-control-allow-origin: http://localhost:3000`.

VAL-CROSS-004.evidence: Observed `dev` script in root `package.json` (`"dev": "turbo dev"`). Tested service availability with curl and all services are responding on their respective ports: API (4000), Dashboard (3000), and Scanner (3001).

frictions: During the logout flow, initial clicks to the logout button element reference '@e3' triggered 'locator.innerHTML: Unsupported token "@e3" while parsing css selector "@e3"' and network/resource errors 'Failed to read: Resource temporarily unavailable (os error 35)'. The issue resolved by reopening the dashboard page and retrieving a new snapshot element reference.
summary: Successfully verified all 4 cross-area assertions. Verified full authentication round-trip via the UI, ensured API consumes shared types and resolves properly during build, confirmed cross-origin requests capability on the API, and verified the dev script concurrent service execution.
